response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer, but his professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments over a decade ago, while the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise for industrial automation. His career centers on web and full-stack work (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), with no evidence of MQTT, IEC 62443/SDL practice, industrial protocols, or message-broker-based C++ systems. The low-latency DLang work shows relevant systems instincts, but the core technical match is too weak for this position.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, and long-term open-source maintenance
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and dojos
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, ended 2015) against a core C++/STL requirement
No industrial automation, IoT/MQTT, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle background
Recent decade focused on web/full-stack stacks, suggesting a significant reskilling ramp for this role
